title: On Call Scheduling Software | Managed Service | Scheduling Wizard
description: On call scheduling software delivered as a managed service. Get optimized, ACGME-compliant schedules built for you and ready to upload to Amion or QGenda.
h1: The On Call Scheduling Software That Runs Itself
paragraph: Eliminate manual errors and wasted time. Scheduling Wizard delivers optimized on-call rosters as simple Excel files for easy upload to Amion or QGenda.

cta.title: Stop Building On Call Schedules by Hand
cta.description: Join 18 departments across 13 hospitals who already trust Scheduling Wizard with their on call scheduling.

faq.answer: On call scheduling software automates the creation and distribution of on-call shifts for medical residents and fellows. Traditional tools are self-service platforms that programs operate themselves, while Scheduling Wizard takes a different approach as a fully managed service — programs submit their constraints and receive finished, ACGME-compliant on call schedules delivered as Excel files, with no software for the team to learn or operate.
faq.question: What is on call scheduling software for residency programs?

faq.answer: ACGME duty hour compliance is mathematically enforced at the moment the schedule is generated, not manually reviewed after the fact. The engine applies the 80-hour weekly limit, 10-hour minimum rest between shifts, and the 24-hour maximum shift length rules automatically. Subspecialty-specific ACGME regulations are built directly into the engine, so programs do not need to manually verify compliance for their specialty.
faq.question: How does Scheduling Wizard ensure ACGME compliance in on call schedules?

faq.answer: Most on call scheduling software requires your team to learn and operate the platform, manually input constraints, and troubleshoot compliance issues. Scheduling Wizard is a managed service — your program submits constraints and receives finished schedules. There is no software to learn and no annual retraining when chief residents rotate. Your finished schedules are delivered as Excel files ready to upload to viewing tools like Amion or QGenda.
faq.question: How is Scheduling Wizard different from self-service on call scheduling software?

faq.answer: Yes. Many Scheduling Wizard clients continue using Amion or QGenda for day-to-day schedule viewing and display. Scheduling Wizard handles the schedule creation and optimization, delivering the finished on call schedule as an Excel file that you upload directly into your existing viewing platform. The two tools serve different functions and are designed to complement each other.
faq.question: Can Scheduling Wizard work alongside Amion or QGenda?

faq.answer: Call, weekend, and holiday burden is distributed algorithmically across all residents so that each person carries an equitable share. The optimization engine factors in individual constraints such as vacation requests, conference dates, and moonlighting restrictions, then balances the remaining call load mathematically. This eliminates the manual auditing and fairness disputes that are common with spreadsheet-based or self-service scheduling approaches.
faq.question: How does Scheduling Wizard handle fairness in on call schedule distribution?

faq.answer: Scheduling Wizard delivers Block and Rotation schedules, Call schedules, Clinic schedules, and Attending schedules. All schedule types are optimized simultaneously so that a change in one does not create conflicts in another. Finished schedules are delivered as Excel spreadsheets that programs can upload to their existing tools for day-to-day access.
faq.question: What types of schedules does Scheduling Wizard deliver for residency programs?

hero.title: On Call Scheduling Software That Runs Itself

items.title: Fair On Call Schedule Generation
items.description: Produces ACGME-compliant on call schedules that distribute call burden equitably across all residents, respecting duty hour limits, post-call rest rules, and individual constraints — all in a single optimization pass.

items.title: Compliance Guaranteed at Generation Time
items.description: ACGME duty hour regulations are mathematically enforced when the schedule is built, not manually checked afterward. Subspecialty-specific rules are built into the engine so violations are prevented before they occur.

items.title: Done For You Scheduling Delivery
items.description: Scheduling Wizard operates as a done-for-you managed service. Programs submit their constraints and receive finished on call schedules as Excel files — no platform to learn, no software to operate.

items.title: Algorithmic Call Burden Distribution
items.description: Call, weekend, and holiday assignments are distributed algorithmically so every resident carries an equitable share of the burden. Fairness disputes and manual audit trails become a thing of the past.

items.title: Scheduling Knowledge That Persists
highlights: New chiefs start with a system
items.description: Program rules, constraint configurations, and scheduling logic stay with Scheduling Wizard year over year. New chief residents inherit a fully working on call scheduling system instead of starting from a blank spreadsheet.

features.title: Built for On Call Scheduling Complexity
features.description: Every feature is designed to eliminate the manual burden of on call schedule creation for residency and fellowship programs.

items.title: Compliance Violations Risk Accreditation
items.description: Manually tracking ACGME duty hour limits across every resident leaves programs exposed to violations that threaten accreditation.

items.title: Fairness Disputes Hurt Morale
items.description: When call burden is distributed by hand, perceived unfairness fuels friction between residents every single month.

items.title: Knowledge Lost Every Year
items.description: When chief residents rotate out, all institutional scheduling knowledge walks out with them, forcing the next chief to start over.

problems.title: On Call Scheduling Is Broken
problems.description: Chief residents spend hundreds of hours on call schedules that should take hours, not months.

solution.title: Your On Call Schedules Done For You
solution.description: Submit your program constraints and receive finished, ACGME-compliant on call schedules as Excel files ready to upload to Amion or QGenda. Scheduling Wizard handles the optimization as a managed service — no software to learn, no expertise required from your team.

searchIntents: A chief resident looking for an automated solution to create on-call schedules that comply with ACGME regulations, so they can focus more on patient care rather than administrative tasks.

A residency program director who needs a reliable system to generate on-call schedules efficiently while considering complex constraints, avoiding the risk of scheduling conflicts that could jeopardize compliance and resident satisfaction.

A program coordinator seeking an effective scheduling tool that can handle the unique requirements of on-call shifts while ensuring fairness among residents, as traditional methods often lead to overstaffing or gaps in coverage.

A fellowship administrator looking for a managed service for creating on-call rosters that integrates smoothly with existing tools like Amion and QGenda, because manual scheduling is too prone to error and consumes valuable time.
